E101.name: Riboflavin (Vitamin B2)
E101.safety: Safe — a vitamin your body needs.

E102.name: Tartrazine
E102.safety: Approved; may cause reactions in sensitive people.

E110.name: Sunset Yellow
E110.safety: Approved, but banned in some countries.

E129.name: Allura Red
E129.safety: Linked to hyperactivity in children in some studies.

E211.name: Sodium Benzoate
E211.safety: Approved; avoid mixing with Vitamin C drinks.

E220.name: Sulphur Dioxide
E220.what: Preservative in dried fruits and wine.
E220.safety: Can trigger asthma in sensitive people.

E250.name: Sodium Nitrite
E250.safety: Controversial — high intake linked to health concerns.

E270.name: Lactic Acid
E270.safety: Safe — your muscles produce it naturally.

E300.name: Ascorbic Acid
E300.what: Vitamin C used as an antioxidant.
E300.safety: Safe — it is literally Vitamin C.

E322.name: Lecithin
E322.safety: Safe — naturally in egg yolks and soy.

E407.name: Carrageenan
E407.safety: Approved but debated — some studies suggest gut effects.

E471.name: Mono/Diglycerides
E471.safety: Safe — your body produces these in digestion.

E951.name: Aspartame
E951.safety: Approved but debated; IARC 'possibly carcinogenic' classification.
